text:
BOTTOM LINE — Tech-led broad selloff, small caps diverged. Semis collapsed 4.5% on ongoing AI rotation exhaustion while defensives XLP +2.8%, XLV +2.2% surged, confirming a clear high-to-low rotation as gold and silver also plunged — the tape is repricing growth premium downward and rotating into value and safety.

INDICES — VIX 16.7, below its 20-day MA of 16.9. IWM -0.06%, DIA -0.21%, SPY -0.54%, QQQ -1.64%, SOXX -4.46%. Small > large by 1.22pp. SPY below 200-day MA; QQQ below 200-day MA — both trigger CTA sell signals.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 1.0x, QQQ 0.9x — QQQ volume declining as price falls suggests low-conviction, not panic. Elevated volume in IWM 1.3x, GLD 1.3x, SOXX 1.1x; XLP 1.1x and XLV 1.1x saw volume up + price up = mild accumulation.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLP Staples +2.80%, XLV Healthcare +2.22%, XLRE Real Estate +2.02%, XLE Energy +0.92%, XLB Materials +0.77%, XLU Utilities +0.55%, XLF Financials +0.34%, XLY Discretionary +0.29%, XLI Industrials +0.05%, XLC Comms -0.64%, XLK Technology -2.24%. Top 2 Industries: IHI Medtech +4.69%, KRE Regional Banks +2.82%. Bottom 2: WGMI Crypto Miners -9.25%, DRAM Memory -8.82%. Money rotating out of XLK semis/tech and XLC comms into XLP staples, XLV healthcare, and XLRE real estate. Rotation state: defensive risk-off.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio 1.18, elevated protection demand. QQQ put-call ratio 1.05, also above 1.0. IV skew on QQQ +11.9% signals high put premium = fear in tech. QQQ spot near gamma flip at 610 significantly below current price — not a short-term hedge amplifier. VIX 16.7 below its 20-day MA of 16.9, mild complacency despite tech rout. MOVE index 68.2, near average — no bond vol stress.

CROSS-ASSET — GLD -1.98% sliding alongside tech weakness is unusual — typically gold rises on risk-off, but dollar strength UUP +0.32% is pressuring it. Silver SLV -3.49% confirms metals selloff as growth fears weigh on cyclical commodities.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 67.5 Greed. Top mover: ABT +10.71% Medtech (vol spike 2.5x + accumulation), MP -8.09% New Metals (vol spike 2.2x + distribution), DRAM -8.82% Memory on sector-wide semiconductor rout. IBKR and VRT worth watching: IBKR today +1.16% on vol spike 2.3x from UNH health catalyst; VRT -3.45% from data center weakness diverging from ABT’s medtech strength.
